b:T10bb1,<div class="specification">
<p><em>Cupriavidus metallidurans</em> CH34 is a heavy metal-resistant bacterium that was genetically modified to be used for bioremediation. The <em>merB</em> gene that controls the conversion of organic mercury into inorganic mercury was introduced in the bacterium. The gel electrophoresis image below shows the presence of the <em>merB</em> gene in two strains after 70 generations.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
<p style="text-align: center;">[Source: Copyright © 2011 Rojas LA, Yáñez C, González M, Lobos S, Smalla K, Seeger M (2011) Characterization of the<br/>Metabolically Modified Heavy Metal-Resistant Cupriavidus metallidurans Strain MSR33 Generated for Mercury Bioremediation.<br/><em>PLoS ONE</em> <strong>6</strong>(3): e17555. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0017555]</p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Outline the aims and methods of bioremediation.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>The generation time of <em>C. metallidurans</em> is a few hours. Two strains of the bacterium were tested for the presence of the <em>merB</em> gene 70 generations after the genetic modification. Suggest <strong>one</strong> reason for carrying out these tests after 70 generations of the transgenic bacterium.</p>
<div class="marks">[1]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain the use of <em>Pseudomonas</em> in bioremediation.</p>
<div class="marks">[3]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>c:T6a7,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p><em>Aims <strong>[1 mark]</strong></em><br/>a. the addition of organisms to remove environmental contaminants<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>the addition of organisms to convert toxic compounds to non-toxic products ✔</p>
<p><em>Methods <strong>[1 max]</strong></em><br/>b. physical and chemical procedures can be combined with bioremediation ✔</p>
<p>c. <em>e.g.</em> clean-up of oil spills using bacteria<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>clean-up of heavy metals using plants and incineration ✔</p>
<p><em>Accept other valid methods involving living organisms.</em><br/><em>Do not accept physical methods alone</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>to observe that it was stable<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>to confirm that change was permanent<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>to confirm that <em>merB</em> gene still present after many generations ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<p>a. used to clean up oil<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>used to clean up mercury ✔</p>
<p>b. used after other clean-up methods have been used ✔</p>
<p>c. nutrient concentrations have to be sufficient to support the maximal growth rate of the bacteria throughout the clean-up operation ✔</p>
<p>d. converts methylmercury into inorganic mercury «less toxic» ✔</p>
<p>e. converts oil into CO<sub>2</sub>, water and simpler compounds «non-pollutant» ✔</p>
<p>f. produces extracellular digestive enzymes «lipase» ✔</p>
<div class="question_part_label">c.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/b/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","b"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"19N.3.HL.TZ0.11"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"HL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","3"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"19N"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","b-3-environmental-protection, b-2-biotechnology-in-agriculture"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">c.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
